Thank you Troy. One thing I want to mention again, and we talked about yesterday, like, what we did was really cool, but the coolest thing about it is it's not like a superpower or anything. It's something I think everybody can do in this room, which is the exciting thing. And so, but it does take a lot of work. Like Troy said, you got to bust your butt, you got to do a lot of front loading of work to get that, and then after it's done, it becomes really, really easy. We're literally going to do a webinar once every Thursday and just hang out the rest of the time. So, yeah? What is the problem with that? Depends. There's a lot of ways to do it. I mean yesterday. Nothing was free. We sent emails out. Yeah. So, I wanted a tangent there. I just lost it. Oh, yeah. So, I was going to tell a story. So, because what Troy is talking about is right, like a ton of front end work, right? But after it's done, then the webinar is done, right? You saw it, like, I don't have to read. I can tweak little things, but I just do the webinar. We get paid. What's cool about it is I was thinking about this. Some of you guys may have heard me tell the story before, but my senior year at Boise State here, I was going to school. I was wrestling. My business was happening. So, I didn't even care about school because I was making money. But I wanted to wrestle, so I had to keep going to school. And then, I graduated and I had, or I didn't graduate. I finished wrestling, and then there's like two more months till school's out. So, for two months, I'm just like, I did not want to go. I was like so painful, but I knew that if I didn't get a degree, my mom would never have any respect for me. So, I'm like, okay, I got to just, two more months, I just got to finish off. So, I'm doing my last two months of school. And I was taking a creativity class, and I had a teacher's name. It was Dr. Murley. He was this little Indian dude about this tall. And he was so excited about everything, and he was talking about creativity. And he was doing this whole class. And then, we had this project at the end of the year, it was our final project where we had to do something creative and then do a presentation on what we did. And so, I'm like, all right. So, at the time, I had created this little membership site called the Lost File. Some of you guys may remember that. It was this little membership site that sold public domain products. And so, put all the things together, and I launched it and did my little thing. And this is back, man, how many years ago now? 12, 13 years ago. And put it out there, we launched it. And anyway, so, my presentation was on this thing. I said, hey guys, I made this membership site. I showed how it worked. I showed how process, showed how I launched it. And we got, I think we had three or 400 people that signed up at like $27 a month. So, I was making, I don't know what the numbers are, $10,000, a month. And it was pretty cool. So, it was made this little website, making money. And I showed the whole class that, and I showed my stats, my numbers, and everything, and I got done. And the class is about, probably about this big people. And then after we got done, Dr. Murley, this little Indian dude runs up all excited. And he's like, did you guys hear what Russell just said? Like, he just made this thing up. He's making 10 grand a month or whatever. And he's like freaking out. And I'm like, wow, he's really excited by this little Indian guy. I wish you guys could see him. It's like the funniest thing. He's getting all excited. And he asked the class, he's like, did you guys see what he just did? Like, who here, like, what questions do you have for Russell? Like, ask him some questions. This is amazing. And we're looking at the audience and they're all kind of just sitting there. And there's just one kid, he's like leaning back in his chair, like, feet up, and he raises his hand like, like this. And I'm like, yeah. And he's like, Russell, that seems cool and all, but man, that seems like a lot of work. And I remember sitting there thinking, I just kind of smiled and I was like, yeah, it was a lot of work. But guess what? I have a membership site now that makes me 10 grand a month. And guess what I have to do for that now? Nothing. There's nothing else I have to do. I had to front load all this work and all this effort. And I was like, I look at him and I was like, you know, in a week or two now, you're going to graduate, we're all going to graduate. You know, we're all seniors at this point. And you're going to go get a job and you're going to start at, you know, $30,000 a year and then you're going to upgrade to 40. And then over the next 40, 50 years of your life and keep working, eventually you'll retire. I was thinking, I'm like, that seems like a lot more work to me than front loading it up front, right? But I did one a little while ago, I think it was during the time that Troy was talking about where we were trying to get ClickFunnels launched and trying to get all these things and it was called the unbalanced life because all of us are talking about how we want to be balanced, right? That's one of our big things. We need to become balanced. And the problem with being balanced, that's an ideal situation, right? You want to become balanced. That's a good thing. But to have great things happen, you can't be balanced to cause massive action on something. I was thinking about when I was wrestling, I didn't live a balanced lifestyle when I was wrestling. I wanted to be the best in the country. I wanted to be as good as I could. And to do that, if I would have been balanced and spent at 50% of my time at school and all these different things, I could never have done it. I had to become extremely unbalanced for a period of time where all my thoughts, my dreams were on this thing to be able to be successful. And then when I was done, I could come back and get balanced. For about two years or so before ClickFunnels launched, my life was very, very balanced because things were working and things were very balanced. But then we had this big project come and I knew what it could be and I knew the potential of it. And so to get it out the door and to be able to have success, I had to become completely unbalanced for a while. It's kind of like a slingshot, right? You can completely unbalance for a little bit. It's different and then you can let go and it shoots back and you can come back to balance. But to be able to execute and to get things done, you've got to become unbalanced sometimes for a finite period of time to be able to just then take off. And so for ClickFunnels, it was two or three months of us just killing ourselves to get it. But then now it's done and now it's just growing and now I can get back to a balanced life. My wife is amazing. Yeah, she's amazing. And that's the key. I always tell people you can only be as successful as your spouse will allow you to be. And then after that, the rest of the day, we'll be focusing more on traffic stuff, so how to get people to actually come and attend and register for your webinars. So what I want to kind of go through is just kind of go through the webinar registration process for this. Now, there's two different, keep in your mind, there's kind of two different strategies on this. One of them is like a real webinar and one's like an auto webinar. And conceptually, they're very similar but they're also different in the way that the registration process has kind of worked. So I'm going to show you his live examples of both of them, just kind of sketch them out so you know. The most simple, the way a webinar works is it's pretty simple. There's a webinar registration page where somebody comes in to register and then there's the confirmation page. And then in the middle here, you connected whatever your webinar platform was, external. So I use GoToWebinar, that's my favorite one. Some of you guys use Webinar Jam. Is there other ones out there? I don't even know if there's other ones that people use. But something like that, right? I use GoToWebinar. And so that's the most simple form. I'm going to show you guys more complex stuff because you can add more complex things to make it really cool. But I want to stress one thing. I'm going to show you guys a whole bunch of really cool stuff with the registration process. Don't let that keep you guys from just getting something out there. This is what you need to have. These are the requirements we have to have to launch a successful webinar. The other stuff is stuff I'm going to show you guys to amplify it and make it better. But don't wait to get all the other stuff to go and launch your webinar. To launch your webinar, your registration page, hooking at some kind of webinar software and a confirmation page. That's all you have to have. So you guys promised me that you're not going to launch until you have everything? Okay, this is important. Okay, so that's this. Now an automated webinar, there's a couple more steps in it. And the cool thing about automated webinars is your ability to sequence and to do actions based on what people are doing. Where you kind of lose that with a go to webinar or some third party service, but you can do an automated webinar. So with automated webinar works, the first thing they come to is to a confirmation page or, excuse me, registration page. So here's registration confirmation. So here's the registration page. And I don't know if you guys have seen in the last week we've done some huge updates to the automated webinar stuff inside of ClickFunnels because it wasn't as good and because we're launching our first automated one, we went through and just pulled a revamp. So it's a million times better than it was just a week ago. So this is kind of how it works now. So you come to the webinar registration page and they come to register and they get to pick a date and a time. So they'll say, I want to do, they'll be like, do you want to do the webinar today, tomorrow or Friday? They pick the date and the time. And then one other feature we add in here because in our split test book we found that this actually was one of our best converting things is we give people the ability to watch yesterday's replay right now. So it's like, do you want to watch yesterday's replay now or do you want to watch the webinar tonight at 10 or tomorrow or does that make sense to pick the date and time? So if they say I want to watch yesterday's replay now, automatically takes them to the page where they can watch the replay. Now, I thought that that option would be like, when we first add that, Rich Shefferman was doing a lot of automated webinar stuff, he's one of the first guys doing it. And he had that as a feature on his. We were like, choose yesterday or watch yesterday's replay now. And I remember I like hated that idea because it was backwards of everything we're trying to do with automated webinar. But we tested it and it turned out that I think it was like 25 or 30% of people took that option, but then over 50% of our income came from that option. So the people who were excited hyper buyers who wanted it right now, even though there was a smaller percentage went this way, the majority of our money came from that. So we now have that in all of our automated webinars. In fact, I use it as the default check because that's what I want. If they don't, if they take a date and a time, then it takes them over here to a confirmation page. Then it has like a countdown clock that shows you when your webinar starts and it gives them like their own custom personal URL. So this is your URL, come back to this page on the date and the time and then the webinar will happen. So if they come back to this URL or they're on this page when the clock is to zero and when the date and the time they picked the webinar happens, it redirects over here to an actual page where they can go and watch the webinar and that's what happens. Now the other cool thing about the automated webinar stuff is inside of ClickFunnels, I'll kind of show you guys this, you can set up really cool sequencing so you can say everyone that registers, send them this email sequence. So email one, email two, email three, kind of send that out. Now if they register and they show up to the webinar but they leave early, send them this sequence. You were on the webinar but so you had to leave early and so you can have a whole email sequencing saying hey you left early. If they stay on the webinar and they stay all the way past where you do your clothes and your pitch, you can set a little trigger points that says hey this person stayed till after they saw the offer, then you can send them out a different email sequence and if they never showed up you can send out a different email sequence. Okay that makes sense. There's four kind of tracks you can take somebody on based on what they do here. And so that's what's really powerful about automated webinar. Again typically someone watching it here versus someone watching it here, your conversions will drop. It's just the nature of the beast because they just do. But because there's all this cool sequencing a lot of times we can get that conversion rate usually not as high as this but a lot closer. And then the fact that this webinar can be promoted literally when we launch the funnel hacks version next week there will be people watching it in every time zone around the world at different times, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week and I don't have to ever do a webinar which is obviously that's the dream. So because there's so much more exposure and because of the sequencing you can usually as a whole get the point you're making as much money or more from here. Do you have a question? are on my webinar you probably saw this before where you get done with the webinar and then all of a sudden go to webinar send you an email saying uh the date has been changed the webinar till tonight at seven and you're like that's kind of weird but what happened is all these people who missed it see that and they're like okay cool and then they show up again tons of people who showed up the first time for whatever reason they're like oh the webinar's happening tonight and they show up again which gives you a second chance to close people and so we used to do that a lot of times when we'd roll the webinar over sometimes like what we do sometimes is we'd have this webinar happen let's say it's Thursday at two and then we'd roll it over again that night that seven o'clock at night the next day we'd do like three times or whatever and just keep rolling it over and so when you do that there's a couple things you do one you can go and you can just keep doing the webinar live every time but Saturday however many times you want and then what these guys do um is that they they'll handle this whole roll over process make sure it happens and then they record your entire webinar and then these guys have got like I have no idea actually in my head this is what it looks like there's rooms with like tons of computers and then at seven o'clock they push play on your webinar and they play your video file and people all log in and they watch it and then the video just happens and they just keep doing that throughout the weekend and so we do that a lot of times um and it's it's really cool um this is coming back to Russell being lazy so the best time is um that I found is like Thursday like 8 p.m. Eastern um but I don't like doing webinars at 8 p.m. Eastern so I don't ever do them then and so I usually do my Thursday at like it was convenient for me which was about my lunch hour and then I replayed them that night at 8 p.m. Eastern um and that's kind of the way that I've done it and what's nice about my market is my market is typically entrepreneurs and most of them who work for themselves and stuff like that and so they can kind of schedule that other markets a lot of times it's different um I've got some friends in markets um where their their potential person's working or they there's no way they can get it so they'll do a webinar at the same time I do mine and they get like 20 people to show up but if you do it at night they'll get hundreds and so it kind of depends a lot on what you're doing and thinking about your demographic are they working during the day are they working for themselves or for somebody else um you know and so it's not 100% correlate but the best thing I think is still 8 or 9 p.m. Eastern on Thursday nights is like that used to be And I got this initially from, if you look at, man, probably 10 years ago or so, Jeff Walker started doing these product launches, right? He did a whole bunch of these product launches. People got on and they started doing bigger ones. And the other guys came out like Andy Jenkins started doing big ones and stuff. And these guys are doing like, every time they do a product launch, they're using this process. They do $5 to $15 million or more. And it's been a really cool process. And so one day I emailed Andy and I was like, what's your script? I know you guys are throwing random videos. What's your script? And so he sent me over a script. And this is kind of what the script is. And I've tweaked it a little bit for myself, but this is kind of the process. And so for this, again, obviously we're not doing a product launch. We're doing indoctrination videos here. And so I don't use the offer. My webinar becomes the offer. So I'm not using this in this sequence at all right here. If I was just doing a straight product launch, video forward, then be me presenting the offer. But for our purposes, we're just doing the first three. And then the webinar does the pitch. So video number one right here is called the wow and how. So for me, if you watch our first video, first off I'm doing a pattern interupt. I mean the fun thing with Mandy and giving the whole thing. But my big thing is I'm trying to wow and how. So I'm like, wow, Russell's got a funnel. It makes $500,000 a month. I didn't know that was even possible. I'm showing the funnel. I blew up big things on cardboard. I'm doing the wow and how. So for you guys, the wow and how is like you're trying to wow someone on whatever your topic is. Like wow, I didn't know that was even possible. So I'm not trying to teach them stuff or anything. It's more I'm trying to just open their minds like wow, I didn't know that was even possible that you can do that. And so this is kind of the basic script. And you can go a lot deeper in this. The first thing you leave is the big promise. So for me, it's like, hey, I'm going to show you guys the anatomy of a $500,000 a month funnel. That's my big wow. Number two then is then you do the content. So for me, the content was me sitting in front of the whiteboard showing all the pieces. Number three, be killed or missed. Hi, and welcome. My name is Liz Benny. And about a week ago, you signed up to hear about my life as a social media manager. And how it is that I've created such an incredible life. And I wanted to invite you to actually watch the replay of the webinar. I work part-time as a social media manager. And I earn more than most people do in a couple of years. So I want you to exactly see what it is. So again, my name is Liz Benny. Click the link below. You'll get taken straight off to see the replay of last week's webinar. And I can't wait to hear your feedback. See you later. Is that brilliant for a pre-frame replay? I saw that. I was like, I've never even thought about that before. Anyway, do you want to talk about it a little bit though? Yeah, sure. So I've tried a couple of different things in terms of getting people on live to the webinar. Because I know that that live experience is always better than replayed. And I really do give it my all. I don't know, it's just this energetic thing. When you're live, it just feels better. When you have that live experience, so getting people on live is really, really, really important to me. What I tested out initially was I would just walk down the street a couple of hours before the webinar and actually just shoot a real-time live. I would grab my iPhone 5 and I would walk down the street and go, hey guys, it's Liz here. Look, I'm so fired up for the webinar today. I can't wait. And I would shoot them like a 19-second video. And that's all it was. I would quickly upload it to YouTube. And then I would send out their messes to every single person that had registered for the webinar. And that was really, really cool. I'd tried a couple of different versions of that. And then we used those, we actually did some retargeting with those videos too, where I don't know, there was one where I was talking to my cat and I'm like, I'm talking to my cat like interviewing my cat, like just doing goofy stuff. Like you will find out. I remember one thing that made me feel, well, my wife felt really good because she used to like Google my name and she'd be like, these people hate you. What are you doing? And her like family would Google my name and like, is your husband scam artist? I'm like, oh man, it's really like really frustrating, right? And so anyway, we're at a Tony Robbins event and I was grateful because Tony got up there and Tony's talking about how like, how people are just with him. Like, he's going to try to change the world and people are, are attacking me and hating me and all sorts of stuff. And he said something that impacted my wife and impacted me a lot. He said, look, there are two ways to build the biggest city or the biggest building in your city. He's like, one is you can work your butt off and go brick by brick and build the biggest building in the entire city. The other way you take your crappy little building and you knock down other buildings, all the other buildings around it. He's like, it's really easy to do that and that's what people do. So he's like, you just have to understand that like, it's nothing personal to you. They just wanted the biggest building in the city and the only way they know how to do is tear down other people who got bigger buildings. He's like, just focus on doing what you do and just ignore all these other people. And that like meant a lot to me and for my wife, it was big when she finally got that. So it's important to understand that. The other good advice for this, Dan Kennedy, a little more, not quite as touchy-filly, his, his advice on this, but it was good for me as well. Do you have a website? If so, how's it doing for you? Let's face it, 10 years ago, websites were the greatest things in sliced bread. You could put up a website to sell your product or promote your service. You could buy some ads and if you did it right, you'd put $1 in advertising into your website and you'd get $2 back out. Over the last 10 years, competitions gotten stronger. Millions of new websites are popping up every day. One cost online are going through the roof and the same website that you would put $1 into ads and hoping to get $2 back out was now losing you money on each and every sale. Now, if this is happening to you, then you're not alone. And if it hasn't yet, it will be happening soon. But there's time to face it. The era of websites is over. In some markets, they're dying in slow death and in others, they're already dead. It doesn't matter how much money you spend or how well you design them or what you try to do now, a dead duck is a dead duck. So that's the bad news. Now, the good news is just like how CDs replaced cassette tapes when they died and DVDs replaced video tapes when they died, the website has been replaced by something we call the sales funnel. So what is the sales funnel and how does it work? Well, a lot of that depends on which type of company you own. Now, there are certain types of sales funds for authors and speakers and coaches and consultants and there are different types of funnels for service-based businesses. Yes, that means that all you chiropractors and dentists and realtors are pretty much any other type of service-based business. There are special funnels just for you.
